title: E-07/94
text:
ORDER OF THE ACTING PRESIDENT OF THE COURT
27 February 1995
In Case E-7/94
Data Delecta Aktiebolag and Ronnie Forsberg v. MSL Dynamics Ltd.
Request for an advisory opinion by Högsta domstolen by order of that court of 12
September 1994 in the case of Data Delecta Aktiebolag and Ronnie Forsberg
against MSL Dynamics Limited.
THE ACTING PRESIDENT OF THE COURT
makes the following
ORDER
A request has been made to the EFTA Court by an order of 12 September 1994 of
Högsta domstolen (the Supreme Court), Sweden, which was received at the Court
Registry on 26 September 1994, for an advisory opinion in the case of Data
Delecta Aktiebolag and Ronnie Forsberg against MSL Dynamics Limited, on the
following question:
In a case before a District court regarding payment for delivery of goods, the
Swedish defendants request the plaintiff, a British company, to provide security
for costs for court proceedings in accordance with Section 1 of the Act (1980:307)
on the obligation of foreign plaintiffs to provide security for costs for court
proceedings.

Is it contrary to the Agreement on the European Economic Area – primarily
Article 4 – to require a plaintiff, which is a British legal person, to provide security
costs for court proceedings, when no corresponding requirement is placed upon
Swedish legal persons?
On 21 February 1995 the Supreme Court decided to withdraw its request for an
advisory opinion from the EFTA Court in Case E-7/94 and decided instead, by
virtue of Article 177 of the Agreement of 25 March 1957 on the establishment of
the European Economic Community (the Treaty of Rome; cf. SFS 1994:1501), to
request the Court of Justice of the European Communities to give a preliminary
ruling on the following question:
Is it contrary to the Treaty of Rome – primarily Article 6 (former Article 7) – to
require a plaintiff, which is a British legal person, to provide such security, when
no corresponding requirement is placed upon Swedish legal persons?
The Supreme Court has withdrawn its request and costs have not been claimed or
incurred before the EFTA Court.
On those grounds,
THE ACTING PRESIDENT OF THE COURT
hereby orders:
Case E-7/94 is removed from the Register.
Geneva, 27 February 1995
Karin Hökborg Bjørn Haug
Registrar Acting President
2
